name: Golden Hour
definition: The warm, honey-colored light you get just after sunrise or before sunset. It makes everything look softer, kinder, and more expensive.
when_to_use: Use when you want instant warmth and beauty with one word. If a prompt feels flat, this is the first thing to try.
prompt_example: "portrait of a gardener, golden hour light"

name: Rim Light
definition: A thin outline of light around your subject's edges, like they're glowing from behind. Separates the subject from the background beautifully.
when_to_use: Use when your subject blends into the background, or you want a cinematic, polished feel.
prompt_example: "black cat on a fence, rim light, dusk"

name: Chiaroscuro
definition: An old painting term for dramatic light against deep shadow. Think candlelit faces in dark rooms, Rembrandt style.
when_to_use: Use for moody portraits, mystery, and drama. Not for cheerful scenes; it will darken everything.

name: Overcast Light
definition: The soft, even light of a cloudy day. No harsh shadows, no squinting. Very flattering and calm.
when_to_use: Use for gentle, honest scenes and soft product shots. Great when harsh shadows keep ruining your image.
prompt_example: "woman reading on a porch, overcast light"

name: Neon Glow
definition: Electric, colorful light like city signs at night. Pinks, blues, and purples bouncing off wet streets and faces.
when_to_use: Use for nightlife, retro-future vibes, or anything that should feel electric and modern.

name: Candlelight
definition: Warm, flickering, intimate light from a small flame. Everything feels closer and quieter.
when_to_use: Use for cozy, romantic, or historical scenes. It naturally darkens the edges of the frame.

name: Volumetric Light
definition: Light you can see traveling through the air, like sunbeams through dust or fog. Adds instant atmosphere and depth.
when_to_use: Use when a scene feels empty; visible light rays fill space beautifully. Forests, churches, and windows love this.
prompt_example: "old library, volumetric light through tall windows"

name: Bioluminescence
definition: Natural glow from living things, like glowing mushrooms, plankton, or deep-sea creatures. Magical and otherworldly.
when_to_use: Use for fantasy scenes, night nature, and anything that should feel softly magical without being cartoonish.

name: Weathered
definition: Aged by time and the elements. Peeling paint, worn wood, soft rust. Surfaces that have lived a life.
when_to_use: Use to add character and honesty to objects and places. New and shiny often reads as fake; weathered reads as real.
prompt_example: "weathered fishing boat on a grey beach"

name: Iridescent
definition: A surface that shifts colors as it catches light, like soap bubbles, beetle shells, or oil on water.
when_to_use: Use for magic, luxury, and dreamlike surfaces. A little goes a long way.

name: Velvet
definition: Deep, soft, light-absorbing fabric texture. Rich and touchable, with gentle sheen on the folds.
when_to_use: Use for luxury, warmth, and depth, especially in dark and moody scenes.
prompt_example: "emerald velvet armchair in a dim study"

name: Cracked Porcelain
definition: Fine, delicate cracks across a smooth ceramic surface, like an antique teacup. Fragile beauty.
when_to_use: Use for elegance with a hint of sadness or age. Beautiful on faces in surreal art.
prompt_example: "porcelain doll face with fine cracks, soft light"

name: Brushed Metal
definition: Metal with a fine, satiny grain instead of a mirror shine. Modern, clean, industrial.
when_to_use: Use for product shots, tech, and modern interiors when chrome feels too flashy.
prompt_example: "brushed metal coffee machine, morning kitchen light"

name: Moss Covered
definition: Soft green growth blanketing stone, wood, or forgotten things. Nature quietly taking over.
when_to_use: Use for enchanted forests, ruins, and anything that should feel ancient and alive.
prompt_example: "moss covered stone stairway into a forest"

name: Watercolor
definition: Soft, translucent paint that bleeds and blooms on paper. Edges melt; colors breathe.
when_to_use: Use for gentle, emotional, storybook feels. Terrible for sharp detail; wonderful for mood.
prompt_example: "watercolor painting of a rainy street market"

name: Oil Painting
definition: Rich, layered classical paint with visible brushwork and deep color. The museum look.
when_to_use: Use for portraits, drama, and timelessness. Instantly makes a scene feel important.
prompt_example: "oil painting of a woman with pink hair, dark background"

name: Papercraft
definition: Scenes built from cut, folded, and layered paper. Handmade, dimensional, and charming.
when_to_use: Use for playful explainers, craft aesthetics, and gentle 3D without computer-render coldness.
prompt_example: "papercraft diorama of a lighthouse in a storm"

name: Claymation
definition: The look of hand-molded clay characters, with fingerprint softness and stop-motion charm.
when_to_use: Use for friendly characters and nostalgic, handmade animation vibes.

name: Charcoal Sketch
definition: Loose, smudgy black drawing with rough strokes and soft shading. Raw and expressive.
when_to_use: Use for emotion, movement studies, and dramatic portraits where polish would kill the feeling.

name: Stained Glass
definition: Bold shapes outlined in dark lead, filled with glowing colored glass. Light becomes the artwork.
when_to_use: Use for spiritual, ornate, or fairytale imagery. Strong silhouettes work best.
prompt_example: "stained glass window of a fox in a moonlit forest"

name: Fog
definition: Thick soft mist that hides distance and mutes color. The world becomes quiet and mysterious.
when_to_use: Use to simplify busy scenes, add mystery, or make light rays visible.

name: Golden Autumn
definition: Peak fall: amber leaves, low warm sun, long shadows. Cozy and a little nostalgic.
when_to_use: Use for warmth, harvest scenes, and gentle melancholy.
prompt_example: "golden autumn park, falling leaves, long shadows"

name: Monsoon Rain
definition: Heavy, dramatic, warm rain. Sheets of water, glossy streets, life carrying on under umbrellas.
when_to_use: Use for drama and atmosphere in city or tropical scenes. Reflections double the beauty.
prompt_example: "market street in monsoon rain, umbrellas glowing"

name: First Snow
definition: Light, fresh snowfall that hasn't been walked through yet. Clean, quiet, hopeful.
when_to_use: Use for winter softness without harshness, and for scenes about new beginnings.
prompt_example: "red postbox under first snow, early morning"

name: Heat Haze
definition: The shimmery, wavy air above hot roads and deserts. You can feel the temperature.
when_to_use: Use for deserts, summer roads, and tension. It adds motion to still scenes.
prompt_example: "empty desert highway, heat haze on the horizon"

name: Dreamscape
definition: A landscape built from dream logic: floating islands, impossible skies, soft physics.
when_to_use: Use when reality is optional. Great for surreal art and emotional storytelling.
prompt_example: "pastel dreamscape with floating staircases and slow clouds"

name: Cityscape
definition: A wide view of a city: rooftops, lights, layers of buildings stacked to the horizon.
when_to_use: Use for establishing shots, urban energy, and scale.
prompt_example: "rainy cityscape at blue hour, glowing windows"

name: Seascape
definition: The ocean as the subject: waves, horizon, weather, and light on water.
when_to_use: Use for calm, power, or loneliness. The mood of the water is the mood of the image.

name: Microscape
definition: A tiny world shot like a landscape: moss becomes forest, dew becomes lakes.
when_to_use: Use for wonder and fresh perspective. Familiar things become magical at ant scale.
prompt_example: "microscape of a mossy log, dew drops like ponds"

name: Cloudscape
definition: The sky as the whole scene: towering clouds, light beams, endless space.
when_to_use: Use for freedom, spirituality, and scale. Beautiful backdrop for flying subjects.

name: Art Deco Era
definition: The glamorous 1920s-30s look: geometric patterns, gold and black, luxury and symmetry.
when_to_use: Use for elegance, posters, architecture, and Gatsby-flavored glamour.
prompt_example: "art deco hotel lobby, gold and black, symmetrical"

name: Victorian Era
definition: The 1800s look: ornate detail, dark wood, lace, gas lamps, and formality with secrets.
when_to_use: Use for gothic romance, period portraits, and moody interiors.
prompt_example: "victorian era study, gas lamp, leather books"

name: Retro 70s
definition: Warm oranges and browns, funky patterns, film grain, and sun-faded optimism.
when_to_use: Use for nostalgia, music culture, and warm analog vibes.

name: Y2K
definition: Late 90s to early 2000s: glossy plastic, chrome, bubble shapes, digital optimism.
when_to_use: Use for playful nostalgia and fashion-forward content aimed at millennial memory lanes.
prompt_example: "y2k desktop setup, translucent plastic, chrome accents"

name: Jewel Tones
definition: Deep, saturated colors named after gems: emerald, ruby, sapphire, amethyst. Rich and confident.
when_to_use: Use for luxury, drama, and depth. They love dark backgrounds and velvet textures.

name: Pastel Palette
definition: Soft, milky, gentle colors: baby pink, mint, lavender, cream. Sweet and calming.
when_to_use: Use for gentle, dreamy, friendly content. The default palette of soft aesthetics.

name: Muted Palette
definition: Colors with the volume turned down: dusty, greyed, quiet tones. Sophisticated and calm.
when_to_use: Use when bright colors feel cheap or loud. Editorial and interior content loves this.
prompt_example: "muted palette living room, linen and clay tones"

name: Teal and Orange
definition: The classic movie color combo: warm skin tones against cool teal shadows. Instantly cinematic.
when_to_use: Use for cinematic stills and action scenes. It is the fastest route to a film look.
prompt_example: "teal and orange street scene, cinematic still"

name: Macro Photography
definition: Extreme close-up photography where tiny things fill the frame: insect eyes, dew drops, fabric weave.
when_to_use: Use for detail, texture, and wonder. Backgrounds melt into soft blur automatically.
prompt_example: "macro photography of frost on a leaf"

name: Long Exposure
definition: A slow camera that turns motion into smooth streaks: silky waterfalls, light trails, ghost crowds.
when_to_use: Use for movement, time, and calm. Water and traffic become magic.
prompt_example: "long exposure waterfall in a dark forest"

name: Candid
definition: Unposed, caught-in-the-moment photography. Real expressions, imperfect framing, honest life.
when_to_use: Use when posed images feel stiff. Perfect for storytelling and documentary feels.
prompt_example: "candid photo of friends laughing at a kitchen table"

name: Editorial
definition: Magazine-style photography: intentional, styled, confident. Every element placed on purpose.
when_to_use: Use for fashion, products, and brand imagery that should feel premium.
prompt_example: "editorial photo of a woman in a structured coat, studio"

name: Film Photography
definition: The analog look: gentle grain, imperfect color, soft highlights. Warmth digital can't fake.
when_to_use: Use for nostalgia and humanity. Makes AI images feel less AI.
prompt_example: "film photography of a seaside town, slight grain"

name: Ethereal
definition: Light, airy, and not quite of this world. Glow, softness, and a floating feeling.
when_to_use: Use to lift any scene toward the magical. One of the strongest single-word mood shifters.

name: Ominous
definition: Something bad is coming. Heavy skies, long shadows, and quiet tension.
when_to_use: Use for thrillers, storms, and drama. It darkens mood without adding gore.
prompt_example: "ominous farmhouse under a green storm sky"

name: Opulent
definition: Extreme, unapologetic luxury. Gold, marble, abundance stacked on abundance.
when_to_use: Use for wealth, fantasy palaces, and maximalist beauty.
prompt_example: "opulent banquet table, gold candelabras, marble hall"

name: Serene
definition: Deep calm. Still water, soft light, nothing rushing anywhere.
when_to_use: Use for wellness, nature, and breathing room. The antidote to busy images.
prompt_example: "serene mountain lake at dawn, mist rising"

name: Biopunk Rococo
definition: Ornate 18th-century curls and gilding grown from living tissue, vines, and organic machinery. Lavish and unsettling in the best way.
when_to_use: Use for high-concept fantasy, album art, and scenes where beauty should feel slightly alive.
prompt_example: "biopunk rococo throne room, living gold filigree"

name: Ukiyo-e Vaporwave
definition: Classic Japanese woodblock composition dressed in neon pinks, teals, and retro-digital sunset gradients.
when_to_use: Use for striking wall art and content that bridges traditional and internet aesthetics.
prompt_example: "ukiyo-e vaporwave great wave, neon sunset grid"

name: Brutalist Botanical
definition: Massive raw concrete forms softened by wild, overflowing plant life. Cold architecture, warm nature, perfect tension.
when_to_use: Use for architecture content, book covers, and solarpunk-adjacent worldbuilding.
prompt_example: "brutalist botanical tower, concrete and hanging gardens"

name: Gothic Kawaii
definition: Cute meets creepy: pastel bats, smiling ghosts, lace and skulls in candy colors. Wednesday Addams meets Lisa Frank.
when_to_use: Use for playful dark content, stickers, and brand imagery with bite.
prompt_example: "gothic kawaii ghost tea party, pastel and black lace"

name: Kawaii Gamer
definition: Cute-culture meets gaming setup: pastel peripherals, plushies, RGB glow softened into candy colors.
when_to_use: Use for cozy tech scenes, streamer aesthetics, or anything that should feel playful and plugged-in.
prompt_example: "desk setup, kawaii gamer aesthetic, pastel pink"

name: Emo
definition: Early-2000s mall goth mood: black layers, side-swept fringe, checkerboard, eyeliner, and feelings.
when_to_use: Use for nostalgic 2000s portraits or moody youth-culture scenes with a soft heart.

name: Grunge
definition: Worn, layered, deliberately undone: flannel, ripped denim, faded film texture, a little grime.
when_to_use: Use to rough up a scene that feels too clean or add 90s attitude.

name: Fairy Grunge
definition: Woodland whimsy dragged through the mud: fairy wings and lace with dirt, moss, and distressed layers.
when_to_use: Use when you want magic that looks lived-in rather than polished.

name: Fairy Academia
definition: Dark academia's bookish charm with mushrooms, moth wings, and forest-library light.
when_to_use: Use for scholarly-magical scenes: old books, candlelight, and something watching from the ferns.
prompt_example: "reading nook in a hollow tree, fairy academia"

name: Dual Kawaii
definition: Split-personality cute: one half sweet pastels, the other half dark or edgy, in a single design.
when_to_use: Use for character designs that need contrast or before-and-after energy in one frame.
prompt_example: "character sheet, dual kawaii style, half pastel half black"

name: Dolly Kei
definition: Japanese street fashion built from antique European dolls: layered vintage fabrics, religious motifs, dark fairy-tale trims.
when_to_use: Use for richly layered vintage fashion portraits with a storybook shadow.

name: Soft Macabre
definition: Death and decay rendered gently: skulls in pastel, ghosts that look huggable.
when_to_use: Use for spooky subjects that should charm rather than frighten.
prompt_example: "still life with skull and roses, soft macabre"

name: Bubble Goth
definition: Goth cheerfulness: black-and-neon palettes, glossy textures, cute darkness invented by pop artist Kerli.
when_to_use: Use when gothic subjects need bounce, shine, and a bit of bubblegum.
prompt_example: "portrait, bubble goth aesthetic, pastel and black"

name: Pixiecore
definition: Small winged mischief: glitter, petals, dewdrops, and light that looks freshly enchanted.
when_to_use: Use for delicate magical scenes with a playful rather than grand tone.
prompt_example: "tiny pixie village in a teacup, pixiecore"

name: Pasta Art
definition: Artwork built from actual pasta shapes: macaroni mosaics, spaghetti linework, farfalle flourishes.
when_to_use: Use for playful food art, craft-project textures, or delightfully absurd portraits.
prompt_example: "portrait of a cat made of pasta, pasta art"

name: Synchromism
definition: Early abstract movement painting with color the way composers use chords: rhythmic, swirling color harmonies.
when_to_use: Use for abstract pieces where color itself is the subject.

name: Neo Gothic
definition: Gothic architecture and mood revived with modern drama: pointed arches, tracery, contemporary darkness.
when_to_use: Use for architecture, interiors, or portraits that need cathedral-scale atmosphere.

name: Hard Edge Painting
definition: Crisp geometric color fields with razor-clean boundaries; no brushwork, no blending.
when_to_use: Use for bold graphic compositions and modernist poster energy.

name: Cybergoth
definition: Goth fused with rave tech: black PVC, neon dreads, goggles, industrial club lighting.
when_to_use: Use for futuristic club scenes or characters equal parts dark and glowing.

name: Art Povera
definition: Italian movement making art from humble materials: rags, soil, rope, found scraps as fine art.
when_to_use: Use for textured, materials-first compositions that feel honest and raw.
prompt_example: "sculpture of a horse, arte povera style"

name: Concrete Art
definition: Pure geometric abstraction with no reference to the real world; math made visual.
when_to_use: Use for clean, rational abstract pieces and modernist design work.

name: Post-modernism
definition: Rule-breaking mashup of styles, eras, and irony; high art and pop culture in the same frame.
when_to_use: Use when you want deliberate stylistic collision and a wink.

name: Avant-Pop Surrealism
definition: Pop-culture imagery bent into dream logic: glossy, strange, and saturated.
when_to_use: Use for editorial-weird portraits and album-cover energy.

name: Abstract Futuristic
definition: Sleek non-representational forms suggesting speed, tech, and tomorrow: chrome curves and light trails.
when_to_use: Use for backgrounds, branding, and tech-forward abstract pieces.

name: Angura Kei
definition: Japanese underground theatre fashion: dark vintage kimono elements, dramatic makeup, Showa-era shadow.
when_to_use: Use for theatrical dark-fashion portraits with Japanese vintage roots.

name: Afrovictorian
definition: Victorian silhouettes and tailoring celebrated through African fabrics, patterns, and presence.
when_to_use: Use for regal historical-fusion portraits rich in pattern and dignity.

name: Antique Grunge
definition: Aged elegance gone beautifully to seed: peeling gilt, foxed paper, dusty velvet.
when_to_use: Use for interiors and still lifes that should feel abandoned but grand.

name: Artificial Nature
definition: Nature rebuilt synthetically: plastic flowers, LED forests, lab-grown landscapes.
when_to_use: Use for uncanny eco-futurist scenes questioning what counts as natural.

name: Abstract Tech
definition: Circuitry, data, and interface shapes abstracted into pattern and texture.
when_to_use: Use for tech-brand backgrounds and digital-native abstract art.

name: Anglo Gothic
definition: English gothic flavor: stone churches, misty moors, ravens, and restrained dread.
when_to_use: Use for literary-gothic landscapes and portraits with British weather in their bones.

name: Romantic Academia
definition: Academia aesthetic warmed with poetry and longing: handwritten letters, roses on old books.
when_to_use: Use for soft scholarly scenes where the library is also a love story.
prompt_example: "desk with letters and roses, romantic academia"

name: Retro Fairycore
definition: Fairycore filtered through 70s film: warm faded colors, vintage lace, sun-flare magic.
when_to_use: Use for nostalgic magical scenes that feel like an old photograph of something impossible.
prompt_example: "meadow picnic with fairies, retro fairycore, 70s film"

name: Pop Kei
definition: Japanese street fashion in maximum color: toy accessories, layered rainbow, deliberate sensory overload.
when_to_use: Use for joy-loud fashion portraits and candy-colored chaos.

name: Pixel Cutie
definition: Chunky pixel-art rendering of adorable subjects: 16-bit pets, hearts, and sparkles.
when_to_use: Use for game-style icons, stickers, and nostalgic cute graphics.

name: Pink Parisian
definition: Paris dipped in rose: pink cafes, macarons, wrought-iron balconies at blush hour.
when_to_use: Use for travel-fantasy scenes and feminine city romance.

name: Pastel Academia
definition: The academia look with the contrast turned soft: cream blazers, mint notebooks, gentle study light.
when_to_use: Use for approachable scholarly scenes without dark academia's gloom.

name: Party Kei
definition: Sparkly Japanese-inspired party fashion: sequins, tulle, plastic jewelry, celebration as a lifestyle.
when_to_use: Use for festive fashion portraits that glitter without irony.

name: Night Luxe
definition: After-dark glamour: champagne gold on black, city lights, velvet ropes and expensive shadows.
when_to_use: Use for nightlife scenes, luxury branding, and evening-event moods.
prompt_example: "rooftop bar at night, night luxe aesthetic"

name: Neko
definition: Cat-eared characters and feline motifs from Japanese pop culture.
when_to_use: Use for playful anime-adjacent character designs.

name: Mori Kei
definition: Forest-girl fashion: layered natural fabrics, earthy tones, dressed like you live in the woods gently.
when_to_use: Use for soft woodland portraits and slow-living scenes.
prompt_example: "portrait in a birch forest, mori kei fashion"

name: Lilac Femme
definition: Soft feminine styling built around lilac and lavender tones: dreamy, gentle, unapologetically pretty.
when_to_use: Use for portraits and flat-lays that need a signature purple softness.

name: Illustration
definition: General illustrated rendering: visible artistic interpretation rather than photographic realism.
when_to_use: Use as a base style word when you want drawn, not photographed.

name: Cartoon
definition: Simplified, exaggerated character rendering with bold outlines and expressive features.
when_to_use: Use for friendly characters, mascots, and anything meant to make people smile fast.

name: Rough Sketch Line Drawing
definition: Loose, energetic pencil or ink lines that look mid-thought; construction lines welcome.
when_to_use: Use for concept-art energy or when polish would kill the charm.
prompt_example: "rough sketch line drawing of a dancer"

name: Comic Art
definition: Bold inked panels, dynamic poses, halftone dots and action-ready composition.
when_to_use: Use for storytelling frames, hero shots, and graphic punch.

name: Doodle
definition: Playful margin-of-the-notebook drawing: loose, dense, and cheerfully unserious.
when_to_use: Use for pattern fills, sticker sheets, and hand-drawn warmth.

name: Line Drawing
definition: Clean continuous linework with minimal or no shading; elegance through economy.
when_to_use: Use for minimalist portraits, logos, and single-line sophistication.

name: Cybernetic Punk
definition: Flesh-and-machine rebellion: visible implants, wires, dystopian grit with attitude.
when_to_use: Use for hard sci-fi characters and street-level future scenes.

name: City Pop
definition: 1980s Japanese city-pop album art: airbrushed sunsets, convertibles, neon skylines, easy glamour.
when_to_use: Use for retro-cool urban scenes with a soundtrack you can almost hear.

name: Cyber Fairy Grunge
definition: Fairy grunge wired into the machine: glitching wings, LED moss, digital decay over woodland whimsy.
when_to_use: Use for tech-touched fantasy characters and glitchy forest scenes.
prompt_example: "fairy with glitching wings, cyber fairy grunge"

name: Cybergrunge
definition: Digital decay aesthetics: corrupted textures, terminal green, grime rendered in pixels.
when_to_use: Use for dystopian graphics and anti-polish digital art.

name: Country
definition: Rural Americana warmth: barns, gingham, golden fields, porch-light hospitality.
when_to_use: Use for wholesome pastoral scenes and homestead moods.

name: Chic Modernist
definition: Clean modernist design with fashion polish: neutral palettes, sculptural furniture, quiet money.
when_to_use: Use for sophisticated interiors and minimalist lifestyle scenes.

name: Bubble Bujo
definition: Bullet-journal bubble lettering: rounded, bold, friendly letterforms and sticker-like flourishes.
when_to_use: Use for hand-lettered headers, planners, and cheerful typographic art.

name: Botanical Art
definition: Precise, loving plant illustration in the tradition of scientific field guides.
when_to_use: Use for elegant plant studies, wall art, and herbarium moods.

name: Soft Grunge
definition: Grunge with the edges sanded: faded blacks, pastel accents, tumblr-era wistfulness.
when_to_use: Use for gentle rebellion in portraits and moody-but-kind scenes.

name: Whimsigoth
definition: 1990s witchy maximalism: celestial prints, jewel tones, tapestries, moons on everything.
when_to_use: Use for cozy-mystical interiors and characters with strong tarot energy.

name: Yume Kawaii
definition: Dreamy-cute Japanese style: pastel clouds, stars, and fairy-tale sweetness with a faint melancholy underneath.
when_to_use: Use for soft dreamy characters where cute carries a hint of ache.

name: Alternative
definition: Umbrella counterculture styling: band tees, piercings, DIY edge across subcultures.
when_to_use: Use as a modifier for portraits that should read outside the mainstream.

name: Analog
definition: Pre-digital texture: film grain, tape warble, prints with real physical history.
when_to_use: Use to warm digital images with tangible imperfection.

name: Assemblage
definition: Three-dimensional collage: sculptures built from found objects glued into new meaning.
when_to_use: Use for textured mixed-object compositions and cabinet-of-curiosities energy.
prompt_example: "portrait built from vintage objects, assemblage art"

name: Decalcomania
definition: Paint pressed between surfaces and pulled apart, leaving branching accidental textures surrealists loved.
when_to_use: Use for organic abstract textures and dreamlike landscapes.

name: Binary Art
definition: Imagery built from 1s and 0s: code as texture, data as portrait.
when_to_use: Use for tech subjects and digital-identity themes.
prompt_example: "face formed from binary code, binary art"

name: Furry Art
definition: Anthropomorphic animal character art with expressive, stylized designs.
when_to_use: Use for anthro character commissions-style renders and mascot design.

name: Caricature
definition: Exaggerated portraiture that amplifies a subject's most recognizable features with affection.
when_to_use: Use for humorous portraits and editorial cartooning.

name: Kinetic Art
definition: Art built around real or implied movement: spinning, swaying, optically vibrating forms.
when_to_use: Use for compositions that need motion energy in a still frame.
prompt_example: "kinetic sculpture in a plaza, sense of motion"

name: ASCII Art
definition: Pictures drawn entirely with keyboard characters; terminal-era charm.
when_to_use: Use for retro-computing graphics and text-as-image pieces.

name: Mixed Media
definition: Multiple materials in one work: paint over collage over ink over fabric.
when_to_use: Use for layered, tactile compositions that resist a single technique.
prompt_example: "portrait, mixed media, paint and newspaper collage"

name: Found Text Art
definition: Compositions built from scavenged words: newspaper cutouts, old letters, ransom-note poetry.
when_to_use: Use for typographic collage and text-driven emotional pieces.
prompt_example: "poem made of newspaper cutouts, found text art"

name: Typographic Art
definition: Letterforms as the artwork itself: type arranged into image, pattern, or portrait.
when_to_use: Use for word-driven posters and letter-built imagery.

name: Encaustic Art
definition: Painting with molten pigmented wax: luminous layers, fused textures, ancient technique.
when_to_use: Use for richly textured abstract or portrait surfaces with depth and glow.

name: 3D Pavement Art
definition: Anamorphic chalk illusions on streets that leap into three dimensions from one viewpoint.
when_to_use: Use for playful trompe-l'oeil scenes and street-art spectacle.
prompt_example: "3d pavement art of a waterfall in a plaza"

name: Anamorphic Art
definition: Distorted imagery that resolves correctly only from a specific angle or reflection.
when_to_use: Use for perspective-trick compositions and hidden-image concepts.

name: High Fashion
definition: Editorial runway polish: dramatic styling, sculptural garments, magazine-cover confidence.
when_to_use: Use for fashion editorials and portraits that should look expensive.

name: Iconography
definition: Symbol-dense sacred-art styling: gold leaf, flat perspective, figures loaded with meaning.
when_to_use: Use for reverent stylized portraits and symbolic compositions.
prompt_example: "portrait in byzantine iconography style, gold leaf"

name: Child's Drawing
definition: Deliberately naive crayon-and-marker rendering: wobbly lines, wrong proportions, pure joy.
when_to_use: Use for innocence, humor, or emotionally disarming contrast.

name: Wabi-Sabi
definition: Japanese aesthetic of imperfection and impermanence: cracked glazes, weathered wood, quiet asymmetry.
when_to_use: Use for serene compositions where flaws are the beauty.

name: Photorealism
definition: Painting or rendering so precise it reads as photograph; craft as spectacle.
when_to_use: Use when you want painted subjects with camera-level fidelity.

name: Found Object Art
definition: Everyday objects recontextualized as art, duchampian mischief included.
when_to_use: Use for conceptual still lifes and sculpture from the junk drawer.
prompt_example: "sculpture of a bird from spoons, found object art"

name: Object-Oriented Ontology
definition: Philosophy-flavored art where things have inner lives: objects depicted as if they matter without us.
when_to_use: Use for uncanny still lifes where the furniture seems to be thinking.
prompt_example: "portrait of a chair alone in a field, object-oriented ontology"

name: Derivative Art
definition: Art deliberately made from other art: remix, homage, and quotation as method.
when_to_use: Use for style-mashup experiments and commentary pieces.

name: Baroque
definition: Seventeenth-century drama: deep shadow, rich fabric, motion and emotion at full volume.
when_to_use: Use for theatrical portraits and opulent scenes with serious lighting.

name: Zombie Formalism
definition: Slick, market-friendly abstraction; process-heavy, meaning-light, gallery-wall ready.
when_to_use: Use for contemporary abstract pieces with polished emptiness as the point.

name: Figurative Expressionism
definition: Human figures painted with raw, emotional distortion; feeling over anatomy.
when_to_use: Use for intense portraits where the paint carries the psychology.

name: Abject Art
definition: Art confronting the discomforting side of the body and decay; beauty's uneasy shadow.
when_to_use: Use sparingly, for visceral, boundary-pushing fine-art concepts.
prompt_example: "still life of wilted flowers and spilled milk, abject art"

name: Funk Art
definition: Irreverent 1960s California movement: crude, funny, ceramic-heavy anti-elegance.
when_to_use: Use for playful, rude-on-purpose sculptural subjects.
prompt_example: "ceramic sculpture of a hamburger, funk art"

name: Post Conceptualism
definition: Idea-driven art after conceptualism: the concept matters, but craft is invited back.
when_to_use: Use for smart contemporary pieces balancing thought and object.

name: Neo-Rococo
definition: Rococo's frills revived with modern self-awareness: pastel excess, ornamental overload, wink included.
when_to_use: Use for playful ornate interiors and fashion with maximalist charm.

name: Barococo
definition: Baroque drama plus rococo froth: maximum ornament, zero restraint.
when_to_use: Use when opulence should be turned past tasteful on purpose.

name: Rococo
definition: Eighteenth-century French lightness: pastel palettes, curling ornament, flirtatious ease.
when_to_use: Use for airy aristocratic scenes and decorative softness.

name: Algorithmic Art
definition: Art generated by rules and code: systems produce the image, the artist designs the system.
when_to_use: Use for generative patterns and mathematical beauty.

name: Augmented Reality Art
definition: Art designed to overlay the physical world: floating layers, digital ghosts in real space.
when_to_use: Use for mixed-reality concepts and holographic scene ideas.
prompt_example: "sculpture as AR overlay in a park, augmented reality art"

name: Digital Animation
definition: Rendered motion-picture styling: smooth gradients, expressive characters, animated-feature polish.
when_to_use: Use for stills that should look like frames from an animated film.

name: Magnetic Art
definition: Patterns formed by magnetic force: iron filings, ferrofluid spikes, invisible fields made visible.
when_to_use: Use for scientific-abstract textures and liquid-metal forms.

name: Graffito
definition: Scratched-surface mark-making: images incised into plaster, paint, or stone.
when_to_use: Use for raw wall-texture art and ancient-meets-street surfaces.

name: Suminagashi
definition: Japanese paper marbling: ink floated on water, captured as delicate rings and drifts.
when_to_use: Use for organic flowing textures and meditative abstract backgrounds.

name: Gothic Americana
definition: American gothic dread: lonely farmhouses, crows on wires, faith and dust.
when_to_use: Use for rural-dark storytelling scenes and southern-gothic moods.

name: Organic Abstraction
definition: Abstract forms borrowed from nature: cells, seeds, currents, growth without literal subjects.
when_to_use: Use for soft biomorphic compositions and nature-adjacent abstracts.
prompt_example: "organic abstraction, seed forms, cream and sage"

name: Low Poly Art
definition: Faceted geometric rendering from early 3D: subjects built from visible triangles.
when_to_use: Use for stylized game-art scenes and crystalline landscapes.

name: Rinpa
definition: Classical Japanese decorative school: gold-leaf grounds, stylized waves and blossoms, flat elegance.
when_to_use: Use for decorative nature scenes with museum-grade Japanese styling.

name: Harlem Renaissance
definition: 1920s Black American artistic flowering: jazz-age portraiture, bold dignity, rhythmic composition.
when_to_use: Use for celebratory historical portraits and jazz-era scenes.

name: Salvage Art
definition: Art from rescued materials: driftwood, scrap metal, and discards given second lives.
when_to_use: Use for eco-craft sculpture concepts and weathered assemblage.

name: Naive
definition: Untrained-artist charm: flattened perspective, honest color, sincerity over technique.
when_to_use: Use for folk-warm scenes that should feel handmade and heartfelt.

name: Rococo Revival
definition: Victorian-era rococo comeback: carved curves and ornament with heavier nineteenth-century polish.
when_to_use: Use for plush period interiors and ornate furniture scenes.

name: Mannerism
definition: Late-Renaissance elegance stretched strange: elongated figures, acidic color, sophisticated unease.
when_to_use: Use for stylized portraits with deliberate, graceful distortion.

name: Acid Art
definition: Psychedelic intensity: melting patterns, electric color, perception turned up too far.
when_to_use: Use for trippy posters and hallucinatory landscapes.

name: Digital Impressionism
definition: Impressionist brushwork born digital: loose light-dabbed strokes with modern color freedom.
when_to_use: Use for painterly scenes with soft edges and glowing light.

name: Surrealist Pop
definition: Pop-art gloss meets dream logic: familiar icons behaving impossibly.
when_to_use: Use for playful uncanny compositions with commercial shine.
prompt_example: "giant cherry floating over a diner, surrealist pop"

name: Cyberculture
definition: Internet-native visual culture: interfaces, avatars, meme textures, and screen-glow life.
when_to_use: Use for online-life commentary pieces and digital-era portraits.

name: Algorave
definition: Live-coded rave visuals: generative geometry pulsing to algorithmic beats.
when_to_use: Use for music-visual backdrops and code-driven motion energy.

name: Preppy
definition: Collegiate polish: navy blazers, tennis whites, plaid and old-money tidiness.
when_to_use: Use for clean-cut lifestyle scenes and classic campus moods.

name: Biomechanical Art
definition: Flesh fused with machine in intricate detail; Giger-adjacent dark elegance.
when_to_use: Use for dark sci-fi characters and ornate machine-organism designs.

name: Eco Art
definition: Environment-centered art practice: living materials, land collaboration, ecological storytelling.
when_to_use: Use for nature-advocacy concepts and land-based installations.
prompt_example: "river cleaned into an artwork, eco art installation"

name: Ontology
definition: Being itself as subject: stark philosophical compositions about existence and thing-ness.
when_to_use: Use for minimal conceptual pieces with quiet existential weight.
prompt_example: "single door standing in a field, ontological art"

name: Expressionist Architecture
definition: Buildings as emotion: sweeping sculptural forms, distorted brick, structures that feel.
when_to_use: Use for dramatic architectural scenes beyond rational modernism.

name: Folk Art
definition: Traditional community art forms: handmade, decorative, passed down rather than taught in academies.
when_to_use: Use for warm cultural scenes and handcraft-textured illustration.

name: Gond Painting
definition: Central Indian tribal art: animals and trees filled with rhythmic dot-and-dash patterns.
when_to_use: Use for pattern-rich nature illustration honoring the Gond tradition.

name: Haida Art
definition: Pacific Northwest Indigenous formline art: bold ovoids, split forms, powerful crest animals.
when_to_use: Use for striking formline animal designs in the Haida tradition.

name: Hopi Art
definition: Hopi visual tradition: kachina imagery, pottery motifs, desert palettes with deep symbolism.
when_to_use: Use for respectful Southwest-rooted pattern and pottery scenes.

name: Kangra Painting
definition: Himalayan miniature school: lyrical romance scenes, delicate faces, lush green landscapes.
when_to_use: Use for tender miniature-style narrative scenes.
prompt_example: "lovers in a garden, kangra miniature painting"

name: Kishangarh Painting
definition: Rajasthani miniature style famed for elongated eyes and elegant, lotus-like profiles.
when_to_use: Use for graceful miniature portraits in the Bani Thani tradition.

name: Acidwave
definition: Vaporwave's harsher cousin: acid-bright gradients, warped grids, digital hallucination.
when_to_use: Use for retro-digital posters with aggressive color.

name: Tonalist
definition: Late-1800s American landscape mood: soft diffused light, muted unified color, dawn-and-dusk reverie.
when_to_use: Use for misty contemplative landscapes where atmosphere outranks detail.

name: Toile
definition: French pastoral scenes printed in single-color repeat on cream; storytelling as pattern.
when_to_use: Use for elegant patterned backgrounds and wallpaper-style illustration.
prompt_example: "garden scene, toile de jouy pattern, indigo on cream"

name: Naturaleza
definition: Lush nature-first styling: abundant flora, organic shapes, celebration of living green.
when_to_use: Use for verdant scenes where nature is the whole point.

name: Pareidolia
definition: Faces and figures hiding in things: clouds, bark, sockets that stare back.
when_to_use: Use for playful or eerie hidden-image compositions.

name: Karesansui
definition: Japanese dry rock garden aesthetic: raked gravel, placed stones, emptiness as design.
when_to_use: Use for meditative minimal scenes and zen-garden compositions.

name: Mythological
definition: Gods, monsters, and legends rendered with epic-narrative weight.
when_to_use: Use for grand mythic scenes across any culture's canon.
prompt_example: "sea god rising from waves, mythological painting"

name: New Romantic
definition: 1980s flamboyant glamour: ruffles, drama makeup, synth-pop theatricality.
when_to_use: Use for bold retro fashion portraits with stage presence.

name: Fauvism
definition: Wild early-modern color: nature painted in unapologetic, non-realistic hues.
when_to_use: Use for joyful high-saturation landscapes and portraits.

name: Biomorphic Art
definition: Abstract forms echoing living shapes: amoebas, kidneys, curves that feel grown not drawn.
when_to_use: Use for soft organic abstraction and mid-century-flavored forms.

name: Atmospheric Landscape Painting
definition: Landscapes where air is the subject: haze, depth, and weather doing the storytelling.
when_to_use: Use for layered vistas with palpable distance and mood.
prompt_example: "valley in morning haze, atmospheric landscape painting"

name: Chiptune
definition: 8-bit game aesthetics: pixel color palettes, sprite logic, console-era charm.
when_to_use: Use for retro-game scenes and nostalgic pixel compositions.

name: Watercolor Ornamental
definition: Decorative watercolor pattern-work: florals, vines, and scrollwork in soft translucent layers.
when_to_use: Use for elegant borders, stationery, and decorative motifs.

name: Trash Polka
definition: German tattoo style colliding photorealism with black-and-red graphic chaos.
when_to_use: Use for edgy graphic portraits and tattoo-design energy.
prompt_example: "portrait, trash polka style, black and red"

name: Faux Naif Art
definition: Trained artists painting deliberately childlike; sophistication wearing innocence.
when_to_use: Use for knowing-whimsical scenes with naive charm and sly craft.

name: Hyperspace Noir
definition: Detective-story shadow in deep space: neon-lit starships, trench coats among the stars.
when_to_use: Use for sci-fi mystery scenes with hardboiled mood.
prompt_example: "detective on a space station, hyperspace noir"

name: Abstract Romanticism
definition: Romanticism's awe and emotion expressed through pure shape and color instead of scenery.
when_to_use: Use for emotional abstracts that swell like landscapes without depicting one.
prompt_example: "abstract romanticism, storm of color and light"

name: Toyism
definition: Dutch movement of bright, toy-like figurative art made by pseudonymous collective artists.
when_to_use: Use for playful narrative scenes with candy color and cartoon geometry.

name: Orphism
definition: Early abstraction of luminous color circles and prisms; cubism gone chromatic.
when_to_use: Use for radiant geometric abstracts built on color relationships.

name: Land Art
definition: Earth itself as medium: spirals of stone, lines in desert, art at landscape scale.
when_to_use: Use for monumental outdoor interventions and aerial-view earthworks.
prompt_example: "stone spiral in a salt flat, land art, aerial view"

name: Neo Dada
definition: 1950s-60s revival of dada mischief: everyday objects, irony, and anti-art gestures.
when_to_use: Use for conceptual collage and duchampian humor.
prompt_example: "sculpture of a painted tire, neo dada"

name: Vorticism
definition: British 1914 movement of sharp angular energy: machine-age dynamism in fractured geometry.
when_to_use: Use for bold angular compositions radiating speed and force.

name: Visual Music
definition: Non-representational animation-style imagery that visualizes sound: rhythm as shape and color.
when_to_use: Use for synesthetic abstracts and music-driven visuals.

name: Tactile Art
definition: Art made to be touched: raised textures, sensory surfaces, sight optional.
when_to_use: Use for texture-forward compositions begging for fingertips.
prompt_example: "relief artwork of ocean waves, tactile art"

name: Luminism
definition: 1850-70 American style of glassy light and stillness: transparent skies, mirror water, hushed glow.
when_to_use: Use for serene luminous landscapes with polished, brushless calm.

name: Graffiti Pop Art
definition: Lowbrow street-pop blend: playful characters, bold outlines, KAWS-adjacent attitude.
when_to_use: Use for urban-pop characters and gallery-street crossover pieces.

name: Lyrical Abstraction
definition: Gestural non-objective painting focused on color, brushwork, and feeling over structure.
when_to_use: Use for expressive flowing abstracts with painterly freedom.

name: Phantastische Kunst
definition: Early-20th-century German fantastic art: dreamlike, meticulous, quietly unsettling visions.
when_to_use: Use for detailed dream imagery with old-world craft.
prompt_example: "dream city on a hill, phantastische kunst"

name: Rayograms
definition: Camera-less photography: objects laid on photosensitive paper, captured as ghostly silhouettes.
when_to_use: Use for elegant photogram-style compositions in white on black.
prompt_example: "botanicals as rayogram, white silhouettes on black"

name: Memphis Group
definition: 1980s Italian design rebellion: squiggles, clashing pastels, terrazzo, geometry with a sense of humor.
when_to_use: Use for playful retro-design scenes and postmodern pattern joy.

name: Palimpsest Art
definition: Layered surfaces where earlier marks ghost through newer ones: history visible in the overwrite.
when_to_use: Use for aged, story-laden textures and archaeological depth.
prompt_example: "wall of layered posters and paint, palimpsest art"

name: Dolly In
definition: The camera physically glides toward the subject on a track. Unlike a zoom, the world shifts around them, pulling the viewer closer emotionally.
when_to_use: Use for slow realizations, rising tension, or drawing attention to a decision on someone's face.
prompt_example: "slow dolly in on her face as she reads the letter"

name: Dolly Out
definition: The camera glides away from the subject, revealing context and often leaving them small in their world.
when_to_use: Use for endings, isolation, or the reveal that changes everything.
prompt_example: "dolly out from the empty chair, room grows around it"

name: Tracking Shot
definition: The camera travels alongside a moving subject, keeping pace like a companion.
when_to_use: Use for walks, chases, and any journey the viewer should take with the character.
prompt_example: "tracking shot following her down the market street"

name: Crane Shot
definition: The camera sweeps vertically or in an arc from a raised arm, moving between ground level and overhead.
when_to_use: Use for grand reveals, openings, and moments that deserve scale.
prompt_example: "crane shot rising from the crowd to reveal the city"

name: Handheld
definition: Unstabilized camera with natural shake and drift; human, urgent, imperfect.
when_to_use: Use for documentary realism, panic, or intimacy that polish would flatten.

name: Steadicam
definition: Stabilized floating movement that follows subjects smoothly through space, no track required.
when_to_use: Use for long following shots through doorways, corridors, and crowds.
prompt_example: "steadicam following him through the backstage corridors"

name: Whip Pan
definition: A pan so fast the frame smears; snaps attention from one thing to the next.
when_to_use: Use for comedy beats, energy spikes, or hiding a cut inside the blur.
prompt_example: "whip pan from her shocked face to the door"

name: Dolly Zoom
definition: Dolly and zoom in opposite directions: the subject stays put while the background stretches. Vertigo in a lens.
when_to_use: Use for dread, revelation, and the ground falling out from under someone.
prompt_example: "dolly zoom on his face as he understands"

name: Orbit
definition: The camera circles the subject, holding them centered while the world wheels behind.
when_to_use: Use for hero moments, product beauty passes, and dizzying emotional peaks.
prompt_example: "slow orbit around the dancers, lights streaking behind"

name: Push In
definition: A gentle forward drift toward the subject, subtler than a full dolly move.
when_to_use: Use to quietly raise intensity inside dialogue or thought.
prompt_example: "slow push in as she hesitates over the phone"

name: Tilt Up
definition: The camera pivots vertically upward, revealing height or hope.
when_to_use: Use for reveals of scale: buildings, giants, skies, futures.
prompt_example: "tilt up from muddy boots to the mountain peak"

name: Aerial Shot
definition: High drone or helicopter perspective, moving above the world.
when_to_use: Use for geography, spectacle, and god's-eye storytelling.
prompt_example: "aerial shot over the coastline at dawn"

name: Static Hold
definition: The camera does not move at all; the frame is a stage and life happens inside it.
when_to_use: Use for composed tableaux, comedy timing, and letting performance breathe.
prompt_example: "static hold on the doorway as chaos passes through"

name: Long Take
definition: One unbroken shot that runs far longer than expected; no cuts, no escape.
when_to_use: Use for immersion, tension, or showcasing choreography honestly.
prompt_example: "long take through the kitchen during dinner rush"

name: Extreme Wide Shot
definition: The subject is tiny in a vast environment; landscape as the loudest voice.
when_to_use: Use for openings, isolation, and scale that words can't carry.
prompt_example: "extreme wide shot, lone figure crossing the salt flat"

name: Wide Shot
definition: Full body visible with generous surroundings; the scene's geography stays honest.
when_to_use: Use for establishing action, choreography, and physical comedy.
prompt_example: "wide shot of the family kitchen, everyone in frame"

name: Medium Shot
definition: Framed roughly waist-up; the conversational workhorse of cinema.
when_to_use: Use for dialogue, interviews, and balanced everyday coverage.
prompt_example: "medium shot, she leans on the counter, talking"

name: Close-Up
definition: The face fills the frame; every flicker of feeling becomes the story.
when_to_use: Use for emotional beats, reactions, and decisive moments.
prompt_example: "close-up on his eyes as the verdict is read"

name: Extreme Close-Up
definition: A single detail fills everything: an eye, a trembling hand, a turning key.
when_to_use: Use for tension details and sensory intimacy.

name: Over-the-Shoulder
definition: Framed past one character's shoulder onto another; conversation with built-in geography.
when_to_use: Use for dialogue scenes needing connection and spatial clarity.

name: Low Angle
definition: Camera looks up at the subject, lending power, menace, or myth.
when_to_use: Use to make characters loom and architecture tower.
prompt_example: "low angle on the CEO stepping from the car"

name: High Angle
definition: Camera looks down, diminishing the subject or mapping the scene.
when_to_use: Use for vulnerability, surveillance moods, or clear spatial layouts.
prompt_example: "high angle on the child lost in the crowd"

name: Dutch Angle
definition: The horizon tilts off-level; the world is subtly, deliberately wrong.
when_to_use: Use for unease, disorientation, and villain energy.
prompt_example: "dutch angle as the lights flicker in the corridor"

name: POV Shot
definition: The camera becomes a character's eyes; the audience wears their experience.
when_to_use: Use for immersion, horror peeks, and first-person storytelling.
prompt_example: "POV shot, hands opening the old journal"

name: Establishing Shot
definition: An opening view that tells the audience where and when they are before anything happens.
when_to_use: Use at scene starts and location changes.

name: Anamorphic
definition: Widescreen lenses with oval bokeh, horizontal flares, and gentle edge stretch; instant cinema.
when_to_use: Use when footage should feel like a theatrical film rather than a video.
prompt_example: "anamorphic lens, horizontal blue flare across the frame"

name: Shallow Depth of Field
definition: Razor-thin focus: the subject is sharp, the world melts into blur.
when_to_use: Use for portrait intimacy and isolating subjects from busy backgrounds.
prompt_example: "shallow depth of field, her face sharp, cafe dissolved behind"

name: Deep Focus
definition: Everything from foreground to horizon stays sharp; layers of story in one frame.
when_to_use: Use for staging action in depth and letting viewers choose where to look.
prompt_example: "deep focus, argument in front, children playing far behind"

name: Rack Focus
definition: Focus glides from one subject to another mid-shot, steering attention like a hand on the chin.
when_to_use: Use to connect two story elements without cutting.
prompt_example: "rack focus from the wilting rose to her face"

name: Wide Angle Lens
definition: Short focal length exaggerates depth and space; rooms stretch, faces distort up close.
when_to_use: Use for dynamic environments, cramped tension, or comedic distortion.
prompt_example: "wide angle lens, tiny apartment feels like a canyon"

name: Telephoto Compression
definition: Long lenses stack distance flat: backgrounds loom huge and close behind subjects.
when_to_use: Use for surveillance moods, crowds pressed together, and mountains crowding a village.
prompt_example: "telephoto compression, commuters stacked in golden light"

name: Lens Flare
definition: Light strikes the lens directly, blooming into streaks and ghosts.
when_to_use: Use for sun-kissed warmth, sci-fi shine, or dreamy interruptions.
prompt_example: "backlit runner, warm lens flare crossing frame"

name: Bokeh
definition: Out-of-focus highlights rendered as soft glowing discs; blur as decoration.
when_to_use: Use for romantic night scenes and gentle backgrounds full of city lights.
prompt_example: "night portrait, street lamps as soft bokeh"

name: Rim Lighting
definition: Light from behind traces a bright edge around the subject, carving them from the dark.
when_to_use: Use for silhouettes with definition and heroic or mysterious outlines.
prompt_example: "boxer in the dark, rim lighting on his shoulders"

name: Low-Key Lighting
definition: Mostly shadow, sparse light, high contrast; noir's native language.
when_to_use: Use for mystery, dread, and moral ambiguity.

name: High-Key Lighting
definition: Bright, even, nearly shadowless illumination; open and optimistic.
when_to_use: Use for comedy, commercials, and worlds without secrets.

name: Practical Lighting
definition: Scenes lit by sources visible in frame: lamps, neon signs, candles, screens.
when_to_use: Use for naturalistic night interiors and cozy realism.
prompt_example: "bar scene lit only by practicals, neon and lamps"

name: Motivated Lighting
definition: Cinematic light that pretends to come from the scene's own sources, enhanced invisibly.
when_to_use: Use for polished realism where the lighting has an alibi.

name: Hard Light
definition: Small, direct sources casting crisp shadows and sharp texture; unforgiving and graphic.
when_to_use: Use for noir edges, desert sun, and faces with history.
prompt_example: "hard light across his face, blinds striping shadow"

name: Soft Key
definition: Large diffused main light wrapping gently around faces; flattering and calm.
when_to_use: Use for beauty work, interviews, and trustworthy warmth.
prompt_example: "soft key portrait, window light, gentle falloff"

name: Silhouette
definition: The subject reads as pure shape against a brighter background; identity through outline.
when_to_use: Use for mystery reveals, romantic dusk figures, and iconic frames.
prompt_example: "silhouette in the doorway, hallway blazing behind"

name: Match Cut
definition: Two shots joined by matching shape, motion, or idea; the edit itself tells a story.
when_to_use: Use for elegant time jumps and thematic rhymes between scenes.
prompt_example: "match cut from spinning coin to spinning ferris wheel"

name: Smash Cut
definition: An abrupt jarring cut between extremes: loud to silent, calm to chaos.
when_to_use: Use for shock, comedy, and waking from dreams badly.
prompt_example: "smash cut from the scream to the quiet breakfast table"

name: Cross Dissolve
definition: One image melts into the next; time passing with a soft edge.
when_to_use: Use for gentle time transitions and memory sequences.
prompt_example: "cross dissolve from her young face to the old photograph"

name: J-Cut
definition: The next scene's sound arrives before its picture; audio leads the way in.
when_to_use: Use for smooth scene flow and anticipation.
prompt_example: "J-cut: the crowd noise rises before we see the stadium"

name: L-Cut
definition: The previous scene's audio lingers over the new image; sound refuses to leave.
when_to_use: Use for reactions carrying into consequences and conversational flow.
prompt_example: "L-cut: her words continue over his face at the window"

name: Slow Motion
definition: Time stretched: motion rendered at high frame rate and played back slow, every detail savored.
when_to_use: Use for impact moments, beauty passes, and emotional peaks.
prompt_example: "slow motion, confetti falling around the champion"

name: Speed Ramp
definition: Footage accelerates or brakes mid-shot: real time snapping into slow motion and back.
when_to_use: Use for action emphasis and stylish energy shifts.
prompt_example: "speed ramp as the skater launches off the rail"

name: Bleach Bypass
definition: Desaturated, high-contrast silvery look from skipping the bleach step; war-film grit.
when_to_use: Use for harsh memories, conflict, and drained worlds.

name: Day for Night
definition: Daylight footage graded into moonlight: cool, dim, and slightly unreal.
when_to_use: Use for stylized night scenes with dreamlike readability.

name: Golden Hour Grade
definition: Warm amber light of the sun's first and last hour, honeyed and forgiving.
when_to_use: Use for romance, nostalgia, and endings that feel earned.
prompt_example: "golden hour grade, field at last light"

name: 16mm Film Look
definition: Grainy, slightly soft small-gauge film texture; indie warmth and imperfection.
when_to_use: Use for memory sequences, music videos, and handmade honesty.
prompt_example: "16mm film look, summer lake, grain and halation"

name: Technicolor
definition: Vintage three-strip saturation: reds that sing, greens that glow, Hollywood's candy era.
when_to_use: Use for retro musicals, dream sequences, and joyful artifice.

name: Noir Grade
definition: Deep blacks, silver highlights, venetian-blind shadows; crime cinema's monochrome soul.
when_to_use: Use for detective stories and moral shadow, color optional.
prompt_example: "office at night, noir grade, blinds shadow"

name: Neon Noir
definition: Noir updated in saturated neon: wet streets reflecting pink and cyan signage.
when_to_use: Use for modern night-city stories with electric melancholy.
